title: davallia thwaitesii
intro: Davallia thwaitesii, also known as the Tendon fern, is a species of fern in the family Davalliaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and Southeast Asia, and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Davallia thwaitesii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
